Reasoning: The patient's current hospital visit record is overwhelmingly focused on severe, acute infectious and inflammatory processes, complicated by transplant complications, encephalitis, and gastrointestinal hemorrhage. The primary diagnoses include: Complications of transplanted bone marrow; Unspecified causes of encephalitis, myelitis, and encephalomyelitis; Herpes simplex with other specified complications; Duodenitis, with hemorrhage; Nodular lymphoma, unspecified site, extranodal and solid organ sites; Gastroesophageal laceration-hemorrhage syndrome; Acute duodenal ulcer with hemorrhage, without mention of obstruction; Infection and inflammatory reaction due to other vascular device, implant, and graft; Other staphylococcal septicemia; Sepsis; Candidiasis of mouth; Toxoplasmosis of other specified sites; Septicemia due to pseudomonas; Hemorrhage, unspecified; Hyposmolality and/or hyponatremia; Unspecified sinusitis (chronic); Unspecified disorder of liver; Acute laryngitis without mention of obstruction; Conjunctivitis, unspecified; Other esophagitis; Ulcer of esophagus with bleeding; Other specified procedures as the cause of abnormal reaction of patient, or of later complication, without mention of misadventure at time of procedure; Accidents occurring in residential institution; Other malaise and fatigue; Late
